description: In 2012, Kansas Governor Sam Brownback signed the Kansas Senate Bill 347, implementing massive tax cuts on pass-through business income, promising an 'economic shot of adrenaline.' By 2017, the state faced severe budget deficits, crumbling infrastructure, and a lack of the promised growth, forcing a bipartisan legislative reversal to repeal the cuts.

key_events.description: Governor Brownback signs the tax cut bill into law with significant fanfare.
State credit rating downgraded by Moody's due to persistent budget shortfalls.
Kansas schools close early due to lack of funds, sparking public outrage.
State legislature passes a bill to reverse tax cuts, overriding the Governor's veto.

calibration_notes: This scenario captures the lifecycle of a high-conviction economic policy that encounters severe reality-based friction, illustrating how public opinion shifts as initial political optimism is replaced by material fiscal consequences.
